description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline using the docker:latest image and a before_script that exports VERSION from CI_COMMIT_TAG or CI_COMMIT_REF_SLUG, sets IMAGE to $CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE:$VERSION, sets STACK to $CI_PROJECT_NAME-$CI_COMMIT_REF_SLUG, and logs in to the registry with docker login. Define three stages: build, kitano, and deploy. In the build stage add a build board job that runs "mkdir -p public" and "cp board/index.html public" and stores the public directory as a one‑day artifact, a build go job that runs "cd candidates/go", "mkdir dist", "docker build -t go-builder .", "docker run --rm --entrypoint cat go-builder /go/bin/httpserver > dist/httpserver", "chmod +x dist/httpserver", "docker build -f Dockerfile.prod -t \"${CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE}/go:${VERSION}\" ." and "docker push \"${CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE}/go:${VERSION}\"", similarly a build java job (cd candidates/java, mkdir dist, docker build -t java-builder ., docker run --rm --entrypoint cat java-builder /src/target/msroyale-java.jar > dist/msroyale-java.jar, docker build -f Dockerfile.prod -t \"${CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE}/java:${VERSION}\" ., docker push \"${CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE}/java:${VERSION}\"), a build java-springboot job, a build java-vertx job, a build java9 job, a build kitano job that creates a bin directory, builds \"${CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE}/kitano-builder:${VERSION}\" and extracts the kitano binary to ../bin/kitano (artifact bin), a build kotlin-micronaut job, a build ktor job, a build nginx-raw job, a build nodejs job, a build php-apache job, a build python job, a build rust-hyper job, a build rust-iron job, a build rust-rocket job, and a build scala-akka job that builds a scala-akka-builder, extracts the jar to dist, builds the production image and pushes it. In the kitano stage add a kitano job that makes bin/kitano executable, iterates over all directories in candidates to build an images list, runs ./bin/kitano with timeout, nanocpu, healthcheck and load parameters and writes the result to public/kitano.json, publishing the public directory as a one‑day artifact. Finally, in the deploy stage add a pages job that echoes "Deploying to pages" and publishes the public directory as GitLab Pages.
